name: Amaethon
description: Amaethon is a significant figure in Welsh mythology, primarily known as a divine agriculturalist and a warrior. He is the son of Dôn, a prominent mother goddess figure, and brother to other important deities like Gwydion. Amaethon's primary role revolves around agriculture, particularly the cultivation of land and the provision of sustenance. He is often associated with plowing, planting, and the bounty of the earth, representing the life-giving forces of nature. His involvement in the Cad Goddeu (Battle of the Trees) showcases his cunning and strategic mind, as he uses his knowledge of the names of trees to disrupt the enemy forces. This battle highlights his warrior aspect, demonstrating his ability to defend his kin and their interests. While not as widely celebrated as some other figures in the Mabinogion, Amaethon's actions and knowledge are crucial to the narrative and emphasize the importance of agriculture and resourcefulness in Welsh lore. He embodies the vital connection between humanity and the land, representing the power and responsibility associated with cultivating the earth. He stole a lapwing, a roebuck, and a hound from Arawn, king of Annwn, which directly led to the Battle of the Trees. His actions demonstrate a boldness and willingness to challenge even the rulers of the underworld, further solidifying his image as a resourceful and capable figure. He is a powerful force, both in creation and in conflict.
subtitle: Divine Agriculturalist and Warrior

keyMyths.title: The Theft from Annwn
keyMyths.description: Amaethon steals a lapwing, a roebuck, and a hound from Arawn, the ruler of Annwn, the Welsh underworld. This act provokes Arawn and sets the stage for the Battle of the Trees.

keyMyths.title: Cad Goddeu (Battle of the Trees)
keyMyths.description: Amaethon's actions lead to the Cad Goddeu, where he and his brother Gwydion face Arawn's forces. He uses his knowledge of tree names to confuse and defeat the enemy.

keyMyths.title: Son of Dôn
keyMyths.description: As a son of Dôn, Amaethon is part of a powerful family of deities. His lineage connects him to the broader pantheon and their associated powers.
